name: wolfgang-supreme-court-drafting
display_name: Wolfgang Rush — Supreme Court Drafting · Claude Desktop Extension (India)
description: Drafting connector for Supreme Court of India. ACRONYM DISAMBIGUATION (read this BEFORE classifying the user's ask): **SLP-C**=`slp-civil` · **SLP-Cr**=`slp-criminal` · **TP**=`transfer-petition` · **WP-Art32**=`writ-art32` · **Review**=`review-petition` · **Curative**=`curative-petition`. Use the acronym map; do not infer from input content. STRICT SIX-AGENT PIPELINE: Reader → Format → Drafter → Verifier → Refiner → Overseer, producing six named artifacts (case-facts.md, format-shell.md, draft-v1.docx, verification-report.md, draft-v2.docx, final-draft.docx). MANDATORY INVOCATION ORDER: (1) call get_agent_instructions() with NO arguments FIRST to receive the full orchestration script, (2) follow every step in that script in order, (3) save every intermediate artifact via save_artifact, (4) save final-draft.docx via save_draft_as_docx. DO NOT write standalone python-docx or JavaScript scripts to generate output — invoke the pipeline tools directly. DO NOT skip the Verifier (anti-hallucination firewall) or the Overseer (opposing-counsel lens). Reader applies the privacy firewall — party names, addresses, identifying numbers, financial figures substituted with structural placeholders before any downstream agent processes the facts. Zero data leaves the user's machine.
long_description: Wolfgang Rush — Supreme Court Drafting is a local-execution MCPB Desktop Extension that helps Indian advocates draft pleadings for the Supreme Court of India. The connector exposes the six-agent drafting pipeline (Reader, Format, Drafter, Verifier, Refiner, Overseer) and six case-type templates (SLP-civil, SLP-criminal, Writ under Article 32, Transfer Petition, Review Petition, Curative Petition) as MCP tools that Claude can orchestrate from a Claude Desktop chat. All processing happens on the user's machine. The publisher receives no user data. Output is rendered as filing-grade .docx via pandoc using the bundled SC reference.docx that encodes A4 page, Times New Roman 14, 1.5 line spacing, and 4cm left margin per the Supreme Court Registry conventions.

tools.name: list_case_types
tools.description: List all Supreme Court case types this connector can draft (SLP-civil, SLP-criminal, writ under Article 32, transfer petition, review petition, curative petition).

tools.name: get_case_type_format
tools.description: Get the drafting format, structure, and statutory anchors for a specific Supreme Court case type. Returns the full SKILL.md content.

tools.name: get_agent_instructions
tools.description: MANDATORY FIRST CALL with no arguments — returns the full orchestration script with every step and tool call enumerated. Optional agent_name argument returns that single agent's persona instructions.

tools.name: get_pleading_base
tools.description: Get the shared SC pleading base structure used by all case-types (Cause Title to Synopsis to Facts to Grounds to Prayer).

tools.name: read_case_folder
tools.description: Read all files in a case folder on the user's machine and return text content for fact extraction. Supports .md, .txt, .pdf (via pdftotext), .docx (via pandoc).

tools.name: save_draft_as_docx
tools.description: Save a markdown draft pleading as a filing-grade .docx file using pandoc with the bundled SC reference.docx for SC Registry formatting (A4, Times New Roman 14, 1.5 line spacing, 4cm left margin).

tools.name: create_case_folder
tools.description: Create the case folder for this drafting session on the user's machine under ~/Downloads/Wolfgang-Rush-Drafts/. Returns the folder path with an inputs/ subfolder.

tools.name: save_artifact
tools.description: Save a pipeline artifact (case-facts.md, format-shell.md, draft-v1.md, verification-report.md, draft-v2.md, opposing-notes.md, or any input document) into the case folder. Required at the end of every agent step.
